item.title: ⚜️ A Special ESPC25 Moment: Handing My Book to Adam Harmetz
item.author: stationsolutions@gmail.com (rodrigo pinto)
item.description:
Still buzzing after ESPC, I was lucky enough to hand my book Mastering Microsoft 365 and SharePoint Online to Adam Harmetz, VP of Product at Microsoft.

It was one of those small moments that somehow carries a lot of meaning.

When we spend years working with a platform, building on top of it, solving problems around it, and watching it evolve release after release, we also end up following the people helping shape that journey. Adam has been part of SharePoint’s evolution for years, helping guide its transformation with vision, clarity, and a remarkable ability to balance innovation with real-world needs.

item.title: ⚜️ Speaking at ESPC25: Implementing SCIM for Multi-Tenant Identity Management in M365 and Azure
item.author: stationsolutions@gmail.com (rodrigo pinto)
item.description:
ESPC25 in Dublin was one of those events that reminds us why community conferences still matter so much.

From December 1 to December 4, 2025, the European SharePoint, Microsoft 365 & AI Conference brought together a lot of great people, great conversations, and a lot of practical knowledge sharing. I was very happy to be part of it with my session Implementing SCIM for Multi-Tenant Identity Management in M365 and Azure.

The session took place on Wednesday, December 3, 2025, in the IT Professional track, and focused on a challenge that many teams know all too well: how to keep identity provisioning and lifecycle management under control when multiple tenants are involved.

item.title: ⚜️ Book is Out: Mastering Microsoft 365 and SharePoint Online
item.author: stationsolutions@gmail.com (rodrigo pinto)

item.title: ⚜️ Episode III of III - Setup your SPFx development in WSL2 ... with PnPWsl2 !
item.author: stationsolutions@gmail.com (rodrigo pinto)
item.description:
Third of a five-part journey is out !

The second episode, was around WSL2 (Windows Subsystem for Linux), THE tool you definitely need to have in your toolbox .

In this next one, we will dive into what in what is about to become indispensable to support your WSL2 journey …

item.title: ⚜️ Episode II of III - Revolutionize your tech workflow with a single command
item.author: stationsolutions@gmail.com (rodrigo pinto)
item.description:
This is the second of a five-part journey : the first episode, was around awareness of the crucial baselines to get started to drive technical projects\teams.

In this next one we will dive into what is now become indispensable for many in the tech community: its opening up new possibilities, blurring the lines between different computing environments, and providing a seamless bridge between what was once a very distinct and separate world.

Step into …

item.title: ⚜️ Episode I of III - A Technical Baseline
item.author: stationsolutions@gmail.com (rodrigo pinto)
item.description:
Get your curiosity caps on, we’re about to embark on a five-part journey (the first one, more to follow) through something that will change the path of your technical projects, … or at least, I’m hoping it will (for better ! not worse 🤠).

So it begins …

item.title: 📰 News Update ! Stay tuned, some awesomeness is coming ...
item.author: stationsolutions@gmail.com (rodrigo pinto)
item.description:
News Flash !

You might have noticed the blog’s been on a little siesta, it’s all been for a good cause …
Over the past year, I’ve been immersed in a couple of exciting side projects that have been both challenging and incredibly fulfilling…

item.title: 🔐 Managed Identity rocks with PnP.PowerShell !
item.author: stationsolutions@gmail.com (rodrigo pinto)
item.description: Managed identities provide an identity for applications to use when connecting to resources that support Azure AD authentication.

item.title: Scoutman's Swiss Army Knife
item.author: stationsolutions@gmail.com (rodrigo pinto)
item.description:
The Scoutmans Swiss Army Knife is a Windows Desktop app that lives happy on the Windows systray.

It is a Windows Forms “ShortCut” builder tool where we can address direct links to files, folders, scripts (PNP Script Samples also!) and browser Profiles.

item.title: 😰 The case of the missing field ...
item.author: stationsolutions@gmail.com (rodrigo pinto)
item.description:
Its been a while, since I actually had to deal with on-premises SharePoint Farm … although im spent so many years with the platform, im still amazed on how you keep learning … with “legacy” systems. 😁

Recently, I’ve came across a strange behavior in a SharePoint 2016 farm : modified dates for sites, lists, libraries are more recent than expected when no updates were made by users …

item.title: 🚀... and the first post is out !
item.author: stationsolutions@gmail.com (rodrigo pinto)
item.description: Been in SharePoint world since 2003, and within community efforts since 2009, I was always keen to share knowledge, tips and tricks during presential sessions in conferences or usergroup meetings ...

item.title: About me
item.author: stationsolutions@gmail.com (rodrigo pinto)
item.description: The name is Rodrigo Pinto, I’m Enterprise Solution Architect with over than 20 years of experience and a Microsoft Office 365 Apps and Services MVP since 2011. Currently living near Lisbon, Portugal, im focused on architecture, development and technically leading teams in Microsoft 365 and Azure. Been blessed to live eXtraordinary times, meeting and mingle with full blown hearted people, I usually spent my free time with the wifey (she definitely 🤘🏻) & kids, coding, playing guitar🎸, marvel comics (lots of them), movies, travelling and organizing community events.
